findings.message: New runtime service `widgetizer` is defined only in `dev.yml`, a dev-scoped overlay. Per Taskfile.yml the dev environments compose base.yml + observability.yml + dev.yml (and dev:lite = base.yml + dev.yml), while infra/deploy/strategy.sh composes staging/prod from base.yml + <env>.yml + services.yml + observability.yml — `dev.yml` is never merged into staging or prod. As written, `widgetizer` runs locally but silently never reaches staging/prod. It is a real backing service (pinned image ghcr.io/acme/widgetizer:1.4.0, a Postgres dependency, and a WIDGETIZER_DB_URL), not local-developer-experience tooling (Traefik/mailcatcher/DB admin UI), so it should reach production. This is the costly twice-shipped parity defect.
findings.suggestion: Move the `widgetizer` service to a prod-reaching overlay: `infra/compose/base.yml` (reaches every environment) or `infra/compose/services.yml` if it is a battery-style data store/worker (reaches staging/prod alongside the other backing services). If it is genuinely intended to stay dev-only, record that exception in the decisions log rather than leaving it silently absent from staging/prod.
